verdict: All checks pass. Reading is fit for HITL #3 approval.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title. Sidecar parses with all required fields. Exactly the six required ## sections appear in the correct order.

length.notes: 1228 words at 200 wpm = 6.14 min; within 5–7 min target.

citations.notes: All three primary resources cited inline at multiple points. Bibliography entries at lines 146–148 exactly match inline markers [1], [2], [3]. No orphans on either side.

no_forward_references.notes: The reading references the four properties from topic 2.1 as established prior knowledge, which is appropriate. No references to future or downstream topics. No forward references detected.

sampled_assertions.assertion: A bad specification almost always fails one or more of the four properties.
incremental fix means keeping the existing specification and adding specific words to address each failing property
Grade 8 / 80-word rewrite is a complete replacement using plain words and short sentences
Adding vague words does not count as a fix — 'Write a good email' still fails testable
Summarise the article in no more than 50 words — incremental fix example
Full rewrite example: 'List five steps to plan a 10-minute presentation'
Marketing team 'make the article shorter' — content summarisation example
Support team 'be helpful and friendly' chatbot prompt — customer support example

faithfulness.notes: All 8 sampled assertions trace cleanly to corpus sections. Zero unsupported assertions found.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms are defined at first use (incremental fix at L31, Grade 8 / 80-word rewrite at L50). Four properties are cited as established from topic 2.1 — not cold-used. No jargon dumps of 3+ new terms in a single paragraph. Concrete examples precede or accompany abstractions throughout. No hedging language detected. Learner addressed as 'you' throughout. Sentences are short and active-voice.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 5 sentences. The 4-question diagnostic is a numbered list (L20–26). 'How to apply' steps are numbered lists (L38–43). The comparison table (L59–65) uses tabular format. Inline concrete examples appear in every section alongside concepts. Definitions use bold-term/plain-meaning shape (incremental fix, Grade 8/80-word rewrite, four property names in table).

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due-week references, or grade weights appear anywhere in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading stays tightly on the topic headline. Covers: diagnosing failing properties, incremental fix strategy, full rewrite strategy (Grade 8/80-word), decision rule, worked examples, and professional practice examples. The four properties from 2.1 are referenced but not re-taught. No advanced or adjacent concepts introduced without deference.

gold_example_comparison.notes: Matches the 2-1 gold example on all key dimensions: clean H1 start, six fixed sections in order, beginner-first tone with concrete examples, tables for comparisons, short paragraphs (≤4 sentences), all primary resources cited, no assessment references. Word count 1228 consistent with the gold bar's ~1200 note. The assessment tie-in defect flagged in the gold notes does NOT appear in this reading.
